nodes.label: Design Principles
nodes.rationale: Numbers/typography carry hierarchy so the dashboard stays glanceable and honest about parser uncertainty, rather than looking like a hype-coach or instrument panel.

nodes.label: Alias Review UI
nodes.rationale: Embedding-matched aliases are auto-written to the personal library with no undo path today; surfacing them lets the user confirm/reject instead of a silent write-back.

nodes.label: Recipe / Multi-ingredient Composition
nodes.rationale: Saves a combo of foods as one loggable unit, distinct from existing single-food templates.

nodes.label: Weekly/Monthly Digest Notification
nodes.rationale: One scheduled nudge summarizing trend (avg protein, weight delta, adherence) instead of only per-day nudges.

nodes.label: Health Platform Import/Export
nodes.rationale: Apple Health / Google Fit / Garmin sync for weight and workout data, since those trackers already exist in-app.

nodes.label: Configurable Nudge Rules
nodes.rationale: Today's rules are hardcoded constants (fixed hour/macro/threshold); make them per-user configurable instead of one-size-fits-all.

nodes.label: Scheduled Data Export/Backup
nodes.rationale: Automatic periodic dump (SQLite copy or CSV/JSON) to a configured location, complementing the existing on-demand export.

nodes.label: Precedence UI
nodes.rationale: Let the user reorder NUTRITION_SOURCE per-account instead of fixed .env order; exposes resolver's existing first-match-wins ordering as a setting.

nodes.label: CorrectMealItem via Bot
nodes.rationale: CorrectMealItem exists only as a REST endpoint reachable through the web dashboard; add a bot command so misparses can be fixed without ENABLE_DASHBOARD=true.

nodes.label: Family/Household Multi-user Sharing
nodes.rationale: Shared targets or a shared fridge/food library across accounts; auth already supports multi-user, this would be a data-model layer on top.

nodes.label: Photo Storage Policy
nodes.rationale: Where/how progress photos are stored, size limits, retention; no decision made yet, needs its own design pass.

nodes.label: README Rewrite
nodes.rationale: Current README only pitches macro tracking; needs to mention the full tracker suite, auth options, and the parser-tier table.

nodes.label: DietDaemon
nodes.rationale: Self-hosted nutrition and macro tracker. Provider-agnostic, env-driven, feature-flagged. Runs with zero LLM/GPU/API key by default.

nodes.label: Modular Monolith Architecture
nodes.rationale: Clean internal interfaces where adapters translate to/from provider formats; core never imports a provider SDK.

nodes.label: Deterministic Parser (Tier 0)
nodes.rationale: Default mode needs nothing — 15-25 MB RAM, CPU-only. Tokenizer + unit dictionary. Ensures core is usable without any AI dependency.

nodes.label: Embeddings Parser (Tier 1)
nodes.rationale: Fuzzy/typo-tolerant matching with alias auto-learning. Requires Ollama sidecar.

nodes.label: LLM Parser (Tier 2)
nodes.rationale: Free-form/ambiguous phrasing via LLM. Requires Ollama. Supports anthropic/openai completion adapters.

nodes.label: Nutrition Data Sources
nodes.rationale: OpenFoodFacts, TACO, USDA — resolvable from real food databases, never from an LLM (design principle).

nodes.label: Multi-User Support
nodes.rationale: Single-user by default. Multi-user enables email/password, magic-code, TOTP, WebAuthn, OIDC. No household/shared-target mode yet.

nodes.label: Speech-to-Text Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Independent from parser tier. STT decides how voice becomes text; parser tier decides how text becomes food items. Works on Tier 0.

nodes.label: Personal Food Library
nodes.rationale: Repeat meals resolve instantly from local cache. Meal templates turn a repeat meal into one command.

nodes.label: Scheduled Backup System
nodes.rationale: Independent background loop alongside nudge scheduler. Reuses CSV writer from on-demand export for byte-identical format.

nodes.label: Local Backup Destination
nodes.rationale: Writable directory on server, set via BACKUP_LOCAL_DIR. LocalSubdir validated against path escape.

nodes.label: S3 Backup Destination
nodes.rationale: AWS default credential chain. Credentials infra-level, not per-user — only bucket/prefix/region/endpoint are per-user settings.

nodes.label: Adopt sqlx incrementally
nodes.rationale: Picked over sqlc/jet because both generate per-engine code with no bridge for single-query dual-dialect. sqlx.Rebind() covers what s.rewrite() already does for placeholders — drop-in, not rewrite.

nodes.label: Squash migrations to one file per dialect
nodes.rationale: No production data yet, collapse into single 001_init.sql each. Existing hand-rolled runner handles a single file fine.
nodes.norm_label: squash migrations to one file per dialect

nodes.label: Barcode Scan
nodes.rationale: gozxing (pure-Go ZXing port) fits no-CGO stance, matches modernc.org/sqlite choice.

nodes.label: Macro-aware recipe suggestion
nodes.rationale: User lists fridge contents, matching engine (internal/suggest, shipped) finds combos hitting remaining macros.

nodes.label: Hevy workout import
nodes.rationale: Hevy has a real REST API (unlike Apple Health/Google Fit). One-shot ETL, not ongoing sync.

nodes.label: Per-user AI API keys (BYOK)
nodes.rationale: Reuses AES-256-GCM pattern from TOTPEncKey. New user_ai_keys table, own AI_KEY_ENC_KEY for domain separation. Same decodeKey()/encrypt/decrypt code.

nodes.label: Eating-out mode (photo menu)
nodes.rationale: OCR photo via tesseract binary (os/exec, not cgo). Transcript through normal parser. LLM rough-estimate shipped as low-confidence per honest-about-uncertainty principle.

nodes.label: Target auto-suggestion from trend
nodes.rationale: If weight trend contradicts stated goal, surface gentle prompt. Framing must stay observation about user's stated goal, not dietary advice.

nodes.label: Import old logs (MyFitnessPal etc.)
nodes.rationale: One-time CSV/export parser, no ongoing maintenance.

nodes.label: Why is this number here trace
nodes.norm_label: why is this number here trace

nodes.label: Data export sanity check
nodes.rationale: Compare row counts against last successful run before scheduled backup/export. Extends internal/backup.

nodes.label: Rate limit auth endpoints
nodes.rationale: golang.org/x/time/rate, per-IP or per-account token bucket. Same x/ trust tier as x/crypto/x/oauth2 already in use.

nodes.label: Smart reminders from historical patterns
nodes.rationale: Learn usual meal/log times from stored history, nudge before user's own pattern instead of fixed hours. Extends existing rules engine.

nodes.label: Correction feedback loop
nodes.rationale: When /correct fixes misparsed item, auto-feed into alias table instead of separate manual step.

nodes.label: Honest about uncertainty design principle
nodes.rationale: Low-confidence/guessed values visually distinguished from resolved ones. Parser confidence already computed; styling only needed.

nodes.label: No-CGO stance
nodes.rationale: Matches modernc.org/sqlite choice. Avoids cgo bindings for barcode (gozxing) and OCR (tesseract via os/exec).

links.rationale: Graphify knowledge graph exists at graphify-out/ for this project.
Auth endpoints (login, passwordless, MFA-email, OIDC) need rate limiting per roadmap.
Backup runner is a second, independent background loop alongside the existing nudge scheduler — same shape (ticker + per-user check).
Nudged if water behind by afternoon and evening.
Nudged if no workouts in 3 days.
Hevy import populates the existing workouts/workout_exercises tables.
Extends internal/backup with row-count comparison before scheduled runs.
Eating-out mode explicitly skips digital-menu-scraper variant, which is in Dropped.
Same reorg-only split pattern applied to store.go and handler.go.
Both touch database layer: sqlx for struct-scanning, squash migrations for schema consolidation.
Both are one-shot ETL import paths, not ongoing sync.
